Calculation.Text: "Enter a description of the usage of the list"

StyledText.Data: Name your list with an understandable name. It makes it easier when you connect your Page item to the list when authoring.

Description: <<List template::Description>> Number of text fields: <<List template::Number of text fields>> <<List template::Text Label 1>> <<List template::Text Label 2>> <<List template::Text Label 3>> Number of media fields: <<List template::Number of media containers>> <<List template::Media Label 1>> <<List template::Media Label 2>> Number of value fields: <<List template::Number of value fields>> <<List template::Value 1 type>> <<List template::Value 1 Label>> You cannot change the list type after creating items.

ParagraphStyle.Text: Number of text fields: <<List template::Number of text fields>> <<List template::Text Label 1>> <<List template::Text Label 2>> <<List template::Text Label 3>> Number of media fields: <<List template::Number of media containers>> <<List template::Media Label 1>> <<List template::Media Label 2>> Number of value fields: <<List template::Number of value fields>> <<List template::Value 1 type>> <<List template::Value 1 Label>> You cannot change the list type after creating items.

Calculation.Text: "View all page items in this Title that uses this list. "

StyledText.Data: A list can be used for filtering page items on a page. When choosing Usage = "Tag filter" the following happens: 1. Created items will also be available as Tags. 2. When authoring, author can tag page items using the tag function. Don't forget the multiple choice question In order to filter content based on a user choice, you must first add a Multiple choice building block in your flow. The Multiple Choice question needs to be connected to this list. Then it will use the list as Tags. Please not, you can only have save the tag filter choice in one IO in the Title for each List and Tag category. For the user A user launch the package and check appropriate options in the Multiple choice question. From now, the package will only display page items that are either not tagged or tagged with the same tag as the user checked in the Multiple choice question. For all other uses - select Usage = "Default"

CharacterStyle.Text: Don't forget the multiple choice question

ParagraphStyle.Text: 1. Created items will also be available as Tags. 2. When authoring, author can tag page items using the tag function.

Calculation.Text: Case ( Media LI 1::Container file extension = "svg" ; "data:image/xml+svg," & TextDecode ( Media LI 1::Media container ; "utf-8" ) ; "")
List template::Number of media containers < 1 Or Media LI 1::Container file extension ≠ "svg"

StyledText.Data: List backend decides where the list comes from. In most cases a list consist of pre-defined items, created in FAT. In some specific cases, the list consist of items defined during runtime. The list backends are: FAT - default. All list items are created in FAT. Post IO - The list items are created from user input in an existing post IO. Session members - The list items are generated by all members' full name in the current sessions.

Calculation.Text: FAT List::List backend ≠ "FAT" Or FAT List::List type string = "AI Aspects"
FAT ModifierValue @ Title::Modifier id = FAT Title::<Field Missing>

LayoutObject.name: T1

LayoutObject.name: T1 TRANS

Calculation.Text: List template::Number of text fields < 2 Or List template::Number of text fields = 2 Or Not FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage

LayoutObject.name: T2

Calculation.Text: List template::Number of text fields < 2 Or List template::Number of text fields = 2 Or FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage

LayoutObject.name: T3 TRANS

Calculation.Text: List template::Number of text fields < 3 Or Not FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage

LayoutObject.name: T3

Calculation.Text: List template::Number of text fields < 3 Or FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage
SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List::Number of List items )) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List item::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "up")
SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List::Number of List items )) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List item::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "down")

LayoutObject.name: Value string

Calculation.Text: List template::Value 1 type ≠ "String" And List template::Value 1 type ≠ "Number" Or List template::Number of value fields = 0

Calculation.Text: List template::Value 1 type ≠ "Boolean" Or List template::Number of value fields = 0

LayoutObject.name: Value popup Link

Calculation.Text: List template::Value 1 type ≠ "Link" Or List template::Number of value fields = 0

LayoutObject.name: Value popup IO

Calculation.Text: List template::Value 1 type ≠ "IO" And List template::Value 1 type ≠ "IO" Or List template::Number of value fields = 0
List template::Value 1 type ≠ "IO" Or List template::Number of value fields = 0
SetProperty ( "list_item_id" ; FAT List item::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title List" )
IsEmpty ( FAT Tag Value @ LI::PrimaryKey ) Or FAT List::Usage type = "Tag filter"
IsEmpty ( FAT Tag Value @ LI::PrimaryKey ) Or FAT List::Usage type = "Tag filter"
SetProperty ( "caller" ; "List card" ) & SetProperty ( "tag_key" ; FAT Tag @ List::PrimaryKey )& SetProperty ( "level" ; FAT Tag @ List::Level )

StyledText.Data: Number of items in list: <<FAT List::Number of List items>>

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty(FAT List::PrimaryKey) Or IsEmpty(FAT List::List template id) Or IsEmpty ( FAT List::Usage type )

ScriptReference.name: Convert List to Page item List

Calculation.Text: FAT List::Number of List instances ≠ 1 Or FAT List::Usage type = "Tag filter"

StyledText.Data: Convert this Title list to a Page item list

Calculation.Text: FAT List::Number of List instances ≠ 1 Or FAT List::Usage type = "Tag filter"

StyledText.Data: Select a list type that are aimed for usage on the type of block that you have. <<List template::Name>> Number of text fields: <<List template::Number of text fields>> <<List template::Text Label 1>> <<List template::Text Label 2>> <<List template::Text Label 3>> Number of media fields: <<List template::Number of media containers>> <<List template::Media Label 1>> <<List template::Media Label 2>> Number of value fields: <<List template::Number of value fields>> <<List template::Value 1 type>> <<List template::Value 1 Label>> You cannot change the list type after creating items.

Calculation.Text: "Select a post IO in this title"

StyledText.Data: Select a list type that are aimed for usage on the type of block that you have. <<List template::Name>> Number of text fields: <<List template::Number of text fields>> <<List template::Text Label 1>> <<List template::Text Label 2>> <<List template::Text Label 3>> Number of media fields: <<List template::Number of media containers>> <<List template::Media Label 1>> <<List template::Media Label 2>> Number of value fields: <<List template::Number of value fields>> <<List template::Value 1 type>> <<List template::Value 1 Label>> You cannot change the list type after creating items.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "io_id" ; FAT List::Dynamic List IO id)

StyledText.Data: Select a Post IO and match the fields

IO Fields in IO Dynamic List
IO Fields in IO Dynamic List

FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Value type = "List"

Calculation.Text: FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Value type ≠ "List"
FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Exposed Value type = "Text" Or FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Exposed Value type = "Dropdown"
FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Exposed Value type ≠ "Text" And FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ List::Exposed Value type ≠ "Dropdown"

Calculation.Text: "Select an input field to add a condition"

Calculation.Text: "Select an input field to sort inputs"

ValueListReference.name: IO Fields in IO Dynamic List

Calculation.Text: FATversion < 3 Or Not PatternCount(FAT List::List backend; "Post")

LayoutObject.name: Modifier Portal

StyledText.Data: Input type: <<FAT Modifier @ List::Type>> <<FAT Modifier @ List::Description>>

LayoutObject.name: Media filename

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Media" Or Not SubtitleMedia ( Media MV LIST::Container file extension )

LayoutObject.name: Color FM
Text Input value
String Number Value

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "String" And FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Prettified string" And FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "URL encoded string" And FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Number" And FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Sync server path"
FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Value list radio" Or ValueCount ( FAT Modifier @ List::ValuesList) > 2
FAT Modifier @ List::Type ≠ "Value list radio" Or ValueCount ( FAT Modifier @ List::ValuesList) < 3

StyledText.Data: Switch list template - only from multiple choice to choice.

ScriptReference.name: Convert List to Page item List

StyledText.Data: No IO's connected to this List
IO Text variables to IO "<<FAT IO @ LI::Name>>" Create a text variable by choosing options from dropdown menus if you want to display text input from this IO as an ordinary text in any block.

CharacterStyle.Text: Create a text variable by choosing options from dropdown menus if you want to display text input from this IO as an ordinary text in any block.

Calculation.Text: "Enter a name for your text variable"
"Paste text variable string that you will use in the title"

ValueListReference.name: IO Text variable data keys LI

Calculation.Text: "Enter a name for your text variable"

TableOccurrenceReference.name: TF TV IO LI Default text

Calculation.Text: "Enter a default text when text variable is not set."
IsEmpty ( TF TV IO LI Default text::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: Set a default text when empty

Calculation.Text: Not IsEmpty ( TF TV IO LI Default text::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: Set a default text when empty

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty ( TF TV IO LI Default text::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: Use dropdowns to create the text variable you want to use. Select IO data Some IO types have more than one data field to read from. Select data from popop menu. IO Context Select if text variable data belongs to member, group or session. Member: Text variable will display IO input from the current member only. Group: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the group. Session: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the session. Auto: Funkis Player will display member input in scoll lackages and group input in slide packages. IO Output Decide how you want the text variable to be displayed. Do you want only one saved item or multiple? Index if you want just one saved item. Enter which index number Click pen-icon to view the output or to add a manually created text variable (not recommended).

CharacterStyle.Text: : Text variable will display IO input from the current member only.
: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the group.
: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the session.
: Funkis Player will display member input in scoll lackages and group input in slide packages.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "referer_id" ; FAT IO @ LI::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO @ LI::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "referer_type" ; "io_in_list" )
